methods.description: Process for preparing and growing P. hybrida plants for experimental use, including clonal propagation and adaptation to soil conditions.

steps.description: Clonally propagated P. hybrida E. Vilm. plants of self-compatible and self-incompatible lines from the laboratory collection were grown and adapted to soil conditions under natural illumination in a greenhouse.
steps.searchable_summary: grow and adapt P. hybrida plants in greenhouse

steps.description: The clonal micropropagation was performed in the Murashige-Skoog medium; the microclones were cultivated in a climate chamber at a temperature of 26 °C and 16 h light/day.
steps.searchable_summary: perform clonal micropropagation in Murashige-Skoog medium

methods.description: Experimental setup for studying pollen-pistil interactions in self-incompatible and cross-compatible pollinations.

steps.description: Pollen was harvested from full-blown flowers 1 day before the experiment.
For castration, buds with anthocyanin were used (the day before the flower opened).
To avoid self-pollination, anthers were removed from the flowers and the flowers were placed into gauze isolation bags.
The Ac-DEVD-CHO caspase-3 inhibitor was applied and flowers were pollinated on the next day after castration.
steps.searchable_summary: apply caspase-3 inhibitor and pollinate flowers

methods.description: Application of Ac-DEVD-CHO caspase-3 inhibitor to study its effects on pollination.

steps.description: Ac-DEVD-CHO inhibition solution (5 μl; EMD Biosciences and BIOMOL, USA) was applied to the stigmas at concentrations of 0.25, 0.5, 1, and 1.99 mM 2 h before pollination, 2 h after pollination, or during pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Ac-DEVD-CHO inhibitor to stigmas at various concentrations

steps.description: Unpollinated flowers were the control in this experiment; distilled water (5 μl) was applied to their stigmas.
steps.searchable_summary: apply distilled water to control stigmas

steps.description: The material (pollinated pistils) was harvested and fixed 2, 4, 6, and 24 h after pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest and fix pollinated pistils at various times

steps.description: For fluorescence microscopy, the pistils were fixed in acetic alcohol (90% ethanol and acetic acid at a ratio of 3 : 1).
steps.searchable_summary: fix pistils in acetic alcohol for microscopy

steps.description: The pistils intended for assaying the CLP activity and DNA degradation were placed into liquid nitrogen and stored at -70 °C for further isolation of total protein and DNA, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: store pistils in liquid nitrogen for protein and DNA isolation

steps.description: Part of the pollinated flowers was retained as the control for assessing the rate of seed setting.
steps.searchable_summary: retain pollinated flowers as control for seed setting assessment
